verification_notes.market_size: Mordor Intelligence estimate ($10.87B, 2026) cross-checked with DualEntry analysis. Other sources (Business Research Company) cite lower figures ($7.52B for 2026); Mordor is used as primary. All growth rates are within the 39-47% CAGR range across sources.
verification_notes.basis_funding: Confirmed by Bloomberg, CPA Practice Advisor, SiliconANGLE, and PYMNTS — four independent sources citing $100M Series C at $1.15B valuation, February 24 2026.
verification_notes.market_share: Incumbent market shares (Intuit 34%, Sage 17%, Xero 11%, Oracle 9%) are estimates based on overall accounting software market reports; no source provides AI-specific share breakdowns for incumbents. Marked as estimates.
verification_notes.stanford_research: Junior accounting hiring decline of 16% cited via Aiifi sourcing a 2025 Stanford research report; could not independently verify the primary Stanford source URL — flag for editorial review.
verification_notes.botkeeper_revenue: Vic.ai processing $15B+ in invoices annually — cited in Vic.ai company materials; treat as company-reported figure.
unverified_claims: CPA exam passes down 33% from 2021 to 2024 — [unverified, widely cited in accounting press but primary AICPA source not confirmed]
Basis demos sub-24-hour month-end close — [unverified, from company marketing materials]
Vic.ai catches 3x more errors than statistical sampling — [unverified, company claim]
